event_schema.rule: Every abnormal condition that is expected, reproduced, or observed must be recorded as a structured event so it can be replayed and audited later.

cases.expected_signal: HTTP 400 with a JSON error body
cases.failure_oracle: The input validation verifier fails if the request is accepted or becomes HTTP 5xx.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: POST /networks/create with a JSON array body
cases.expected_signal: HTTP 400 because JSON request bodies must be objects
cases.failure_oracle: The input validation verifier fails if non-object JSON is accepted.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: Storage snapshots with negative bytes, free bytes greater than total, or double-counted image views
cases.failure_oracle: The storage and input validation verifiers fail if impossible sizes pass.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: Configured direct executor is missing, not executable, or does not advertise process execution
cases.expected_signal: A pdocker runtime error is returned before RUN, exec, or compose service execution proceeds
cases.failure_oracle: Runtime contract checks fail if the system silently falls back or records fake success.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: A large allocation would cross the configured Android low-memory guard threshold
cases.expected_signal: The direct executor returns ENOMEM-like denial with memory diagnostics instead of letting LMK kill the app
cases.failure_oracle: A containerized memory-guard probe must fail if the guard allows a known unsafe allocation.
cases.gap_reason: The heavy container memory-guard probe is designed but not safe for every host-only fast run.
cases.retention: Keep long-run artifacts outside the fast lane and attach them to the build set.

cases.trigger: Android kills a running build, container, or daemon process during low-memory pressure
cases.expected_signal: pdocker records the last memory telemetry sample, classifies the operation as interrupted-or-lmk-suspected, and avoids stale Up/running UI state
cases.failure_oracle: The OOM survival replay must fail if the operation disappears without evidence, leaves stale running state, or loses the container/operation identity.
cases.retention: Keep device artifacts for release-candidate test sets and attach the operation memory ring.
cases.gap_reason: Host/static classification and non-promoting device-plan artifacts exist; controlled connected-device LMK/backend-death replay is not promoted yet.

cases.trigger: pdockerd is killed while a scenario-owned live registry pull is transferring layers
cases.expected_signal: Restart recovery prunes partial pull state, preserves old tags, rejects never-published or partial tags, and records that only scenario-owned fixture paths were touched
cases.failure_oracle: The live-pull gate must fail if a partial tag/layer becomes inspectable/runnable, a user tag is overwritten, or cleanup reaches outside scenario-owned paths.
cases.retention: Keep device artifacts with release-candidate test sets; planned-gap artifacts are non-promoting.
cases.gap_reason: Timed live registry pull interruption is represented but not implemented/promoted; only scenario-owned or isolated fixtures may be used.

cases.trigger: A readonly build, daemon, or log terminal receives focus or text selection
cases.expected_signal: The terminal remains readonly and does not summon the soft keyboard
cases.failure_oracle: UI action checks fail if readonly terminal metadata or keyboard suppression is removed.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: The user has not granted a Documents tree or the tree refuses writes
cases.expected_signal: The UI reports the missing grant or failed sync without pretending the path is a POSIX bind mount
cases.failure_oracle: Documents mediator smoke and UI checks fail if missing grants are treated as writable.
cases.retention: Keep device artifacts for release-candidate test sets.

cases.trigger: A GPU workload requests unsupported bridge behavior
cases.expected_signal: The bridge returns an explicit diagnostic instead of fake success or wrong CPU output
cases.failure_oracle: GPU bridge smoke fails if unsupported work is accepted without a diagnostic.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.

cases.trigger: Compose or Engine API asks for a network mode outside default, bridge, host, or none
cases.expected_signal: The request is rejected or recorded with a compatibility warning before any misleading container state is created
cases.failure_oracle: A network negative corpus must fail if unsupported modes are accepted silently.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.
cases.gap_reason: A dedicated network negative corpus is not implemented yet.

cases.trigger: A managed pager page-in/page-out or admission path cannot reserve or write app-private backing storage
cases.expected_signal: The operation fails closed with classification pager_storage_exhausted, keeps stale UI state disabled, and persists a bounded diagnostic artifact when possible
cases.failure_oracle: The APK memory pager contract test fails if storage exhaustion is not documented as fail-closed or if UI telemetry lacks classification and storage fields.
cases.retention: Keep the latest managed pager artifact and final memory diagnostic summary; pager backing files may be deleted after summary retention.
cases.gap_reason: The storage-pressure device replay is designed but the C/C++ pager implementation is not part of this contract-only slice.

cases.trigger: A SIGSEGV or userfault event cannot be matched to a valid managed region table entry or cannot be resolved safely
cases.expected_signal: Non-managed SIGSEGV is delivered unchanged; managed-but-unresolved faults fail closed with classification pager_fault_unhandled and region/page diagnostics when known
cases.failure_oracle: The host contract test fails if the docs allow suppressing unrelated SIGSEGV or resuming after an unresolved managed fault.
cases.retention: Keep the latest transparent pager artifact and bounded diagnostic summary for release-candidate test sets.
cases.gap_reason: The negative fault replay needs the implementation; this slice defines the fail-closed contract only.

cases.trigger: A large anonymous allocation opts into the pager but violates size caps, mapping constraints, executable/shared/stack/GPU exclusions, or region-table admission checks
cases.expected_signal: The request passes through unmanaged when safe or is denied with ENOMEM-style diagnostics classified as allocation_denied_enomem; Docker memory keys alone never enable the pager
cases.failure_oracle: The APK memory pager contract test fails if large allocation opt-in, mmap/mprotect/sigaction/munmap constraints, or fail-closed ENOMEM classification are missing.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.
cases.gap_reason: Admission-negative device replay is planned after the transparent pager implementation; static contract coverage is mandatory now.

cases.trigger: The selected semantic+C0+C1+C2 check count is below two times implementation token count
cases.expected_signal: The test-design verifier writes an artifact and exits nonzero
cases.failure_oracle: The verifier must fail and must not pass while the literal ratio remains below the configured minimum.
cases.retention: Keep latest artifact in docs/test and preserve release-candidate artifacts with the build set.
